title: glob/regex cache keys alloc at eval time
description: str_matches_glob (evaluator.rs:391-393) allocates format!("glob:{pat}") and format!("(?i)glob:{pat}") on every call at evaluation time, used only as HashMap::get keys. str_matches_regex (evaluator.rs:442) similarly allocates format!("(?s)\A(?:{pattern})\z") per call. str_matches_regex_pat (evaluator.rs:453) allocates format!("(?i){anchored}") per call when casemap=true. All three allocations are in the hot path (called per pattern per header per message) even though the cache was built at compile() time precisely to avoid this. Fix: restructure cache keys so lookups can use borrowed str, e.g. by storing the regex_str as the key for globs (as cache_pattern does for regex), or by using a two-level map keyed first on is_casemap.
notes: Glob cache key allocation (format\!('glob:{pat}')) is one alloc per pattern×match call; trivially cheap vs the regex match itself. Downgraded to P3.
